goal: Cutover panel with plan editor, step tracker, rollback controls, Go-Live terminal status display, and EngagementStatus type union sync

acceptance_criteria.description: EngagementStatus union type in frontend/src/types/Migration.ts extended to include 'CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS' and 'GO_LIVE' as union members. This is the prerequisite for all other changes — must be done first. Then update ALL files with Record<EngagementStatus, ...> or EngagementStatus[] arrays: EngagementDetail.tsx STATUS_COLOR/STATUS_BG maps (CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS uses coral for action-in-progress, GO_LIVE uses sage for success), defaultTab function (CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS returns 'cutover', GO_LIVE returns 'cutover'), PHASE_ORDER array extended. EngagementList.tsx STATUS_COLORS map extended. ComparativeView.tsx PHASE_ORDER and PHASE_LABELS extended. PhaseGateDialog.tsx PHASE_LABELS extended. PhaseStepper.tsx updated to render the two new phase steps (GO_LIVE shown with checkmark icon as terminal, no arrow after).
acceptance_criteria.verification: cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test -- --run

acceptance_criteria.description: New API hooks in useMigrationApi.ts: useCutoverPlans(engagementId) returns paginated cutover plans. useCutoverPlan(engagementId, planId) returns single plan with steps. useCreateCutoverPlan() mutation. useApproveCutoverPlan() mutation. useExecuteCutoverPlan() mutation. useRollbackCutoverPlan() mutation. useUpdateCutoverStep() mutation for step status updates. All use existing tanstack-query pattern with queryKey arrays and fetch wrappers.

CutoverPanel.tsx component: Props = {engagementId: string}. Shows cutover plan list if plans exist, or 'Create Cutover Plan' button if none. Plan detail view shows: plan status badge, approval status, step list as a vertical timeline/stepper. Each step shows: label, type (AUTOMATED/MANUAL) badge, status (PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED/FAILED/SKIPPED) with color coding (PENDING=gray, IN_PROGRESS=blue, COMPLETED=green, FAILED=red, SKIPPED=amber). Action buttons conditional on plan status: DRAFT has 'Approve' button, APPROVED has 'Execute' button, EXECUTING has step status toggles (Mark Complete/Mark Failed/Skip), any active has 'Rollback' button with confirmation dialog requiring reason textarea.

New 'cutover' tab added to EngagementDetail.tsx Tab union type and TABS array: {key: 'cutover', label: 'Cutover'}. Tab renders CutoverPanel. Tab is visible for all engagement statuses (allows reviewing historical cutover plans) but Create/Execute buttons are disabled unless engagement is in COMPLETE or CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS status. Go-Live terminal state: when engagement status is GO_LIVE, the cutover panel shows a prominent success banner: 'Migration Complete — System is Live' with the go-live timestamp and the completed cutover plan summary below.

critical: EngagementStatus union type must include all 9 statuses — TypeScript compiler enforces exhaustive switches
ALL Record<EngagementStatus, ...> maps must cover all 9 statuses — missing entries cause runtime undefined
Rollback button shows confirmation dialog with reason textarea — prevents accidental rollback
Step status changes are individual mutations — not batch updates
high: PhaseStepper renders GO_LIVE as terminal with distinct visual treatment (checkmark, no arrow after)
Cutover plan step timeline uses consistent vertical layout matching existing PhaseStepper pattern
WebSocket events (cutover_started, step_completed, cutover_completed, cutover_rolled_back) trigger query invalidation
AUTOMATED vs MANUAL step types have distinct visual badges
medium: Go-Live success banner uses sage background for positive association
Step timestamps shown relative ('2 hours ago') with absolute tooltip on hover
Failed step shows error_message in red text below the step label
